# Zap — AI Coding Agent Built in Rust

> Zap is a fast, open-source AI coding agent. It injects only the context your task needs — no prompt bloat. Single binary, no runtime dependencies. Works with Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, DeepSeek, and local models via LM Studio or Ollama.

## What makes zap different

- **Skill-first context**: Skills are markdown files injected only when your message triggers them. A Rust question gets the Rust skill. A greeting costs 31 tokens, not 2,000.
- **AST code index**: Built with tree-sitter + SQLite. The model knows what already exists before deciding what to create — no duplicate files, no invented patterns.
- **Context visibility**: See exactly what's in the context window. Remove stale messages with `/context drop` without starting a new session.
- **Dynamic MCP loading**: MCP servers stay pending at startup. Tool schemas only enter context when explicitly needed.
- **Multi-provider**: Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, DeepSeek, LM Studio, Ollama. Switch mid-session with `/model`.
- **Single binary**: Written in Rust. Cold start in milliseconds. No Python venv, no Node.js, no Docker.
- **Zero telemetry**: No usage data sent anywhere. MIT licensed.

## Install

```bash
cargo install zap-coding-agent
```

Or download a pre-built binary from https://github.com/zap-coding-agent/zap-coding-agent/releases

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://zap.justpush.cloud/docs.html#install)
- [Configuration](https://zap.justpush.cloud/docs.html#config)

- [Context Visibility](https://zap.justpush.cloud/docs.html#context-visibility)
- [Dynamic MCP Loading](https://zap.justpush.cloud/docs.html#mcp)
- [Multi-Provider](https://zap.justpush.cloud/docs.html#providers)
